parsed_review.summary: CP00-325 is a descriptor-only RP10 (intake-core) closeout pack covering 150 units (RP10.P02.M09.S13-RP10.P04.M02.S07) that closes the RP10.P02/P03 descriptor scopes and adds RP10.P04 UI-foundation descriptor rows. Independent read-only inspection of service.js, registry.js, validators.js, index.js, the validator script, the contract, manifest, command-evidence, and tests confirms the pack-level claim set is honest and supported: production_ready is false (status implemented_pending_review / gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending), no_real_data is true, and writes_product_state is false. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are exhaustively asserted via the freeze helpers and no_write_attestations and independently re-checked by the CP325 coverage and descriptor validators and three CP325 tests (coverage.valid, descriptor-only rows, no-leak guards, authority boundary). Claude is held as a read-only independent reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false); the Hermes packet is a deterministic evidence descriptor with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false and no product-runtime embedding. The Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is a coverage/foundation-gated candidate flag, not the pack-level production_ready flag, so it is not an overclaim. All 7 required public exports exist; deliverable/phase/micro distributions sum to 150 and are validated; the contract current_pack, no_write_attestation, requirements, and descriptor projections deep-equal the generated artifacts; historical_pack_bindings tail is CP00-325. Pre-closeout validation commands are recorded exit_code 0; closeout-pack:validate and receipt commands are correctly deferred as post-review gated. Review-hardening forbidden-evidence list is present, auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, and no invalid attempts are claimed as evidence. Two non-blocking P3 process notes only; no P0/P1/P2 defects. Verdict PASS — does not block pack or goal closeout, and final enterprise-trust/production promotion correctly remains a human approval gate.

findings.title: production_ready correctly declared false with no enterprise-trust overclaim
findings.details: command-evidence.json (production_ready:false, status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending), manifest.json (production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review), and the contract all decline to claim production readiness or enterprise trust from local validation. The Hermes packet's production_ready_candidate:true is gated on coverage.valid && foundation.valid and is a distinct candidate signal, consistent with CP321-324, not the pack-level flag. This is the correct non-overclaiming posture for a descriptor-only pack pending Claude review and human final approval.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Keep human final approval as the sole gate for any production_ready=true or enterprise-trust promotion; do not let the Hermes production_ready_candidate flag be reinterpreted as a pack-level readiness claim downstream.

findings.title: Post-review gated validation commands not yet recorded (by design)
findings.details: closeout-pack:validate, the review run/normalize/receipt:validate commands, closeout-pack-plan:generate, and git diff --check --cached are listed in notes as post-review gated and are absent from the executed commands list. This is expected because they require the accepted claude-review-result.json, but pack closeout must not proceed until they are executed and pass with a clean staged diff.
findings.recommendation: Before committing/closing CP00-325, run the post-review gated commands and record their exit codes in command-evidence.json, and confirm git diff --check --cached is clean.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected by design; verify execution before closeout. Non-blocking for this review.
